methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The market must always hold enough underlying assets to cover all unclaimed withdrawals and accrued protocol fees.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the borrower can borrow these reserved funds, users who have successfully queued and processed withdrawals will not be able to claim their funds, and the protocol fee recipient will be unable to collect fees, leading to hard insolvency.

invariants.description: The sum of all individual account scaled balances must exactly equal the market's scaled total supply.
invariants.attack_scenario: If total supply is less than the sum of balances, the market is undercollateralized relative to user claims, leading to a bank run. If greater, the borrower is paying interest on phantom debt.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_totalSupplyMatchesBalances() public view { uint256 sum; /* iterate users */ assert(sum == _state.scaledTotalSupply); }

invariants.description: The market's scale factor, representing accrued interest, must be monotonically increasing.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the scale factor decreases, lenders' normalized balances decrease, effectively stealing their principal or previously accrued interest.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_scaleFactorMonotonicity() public view { assert(_state.scaleFactor >= previousScaleFactor); }

invariants.description: The total scaled pending withdrawals must never exceed the market's scaled total supply.
invariants.attack_scenario: If pending withdrawals exceed total supply, it implies users have queued more tokens for withdrawal than exist, leading to insolvency and broken debt accounting.

invariants.description: The scaled amount burned in any withdrawal batch must never exceed the total scaled amount queued for that batch.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a batch burns more than its total queued amount, it would underflow or incorrectly reduce the market's total supply, breaking the accounting and potentially stealing value from other lenders.

invariants.description: A closed market must have its annual interest rate set to 0 and its reserve ratio set to 100%.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a closed market continues to accrue interest, the borrower's debt would grow infinitely without the ability to borrow. If the reserve ratio is < 100%, the borrower could theoretically borrow from a closed market, preventing lenders from withdrawing.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_closedMarketParameters() public view { if (_state.isClosed) { assert(_state.annualInterestBips == 0 && _state.reserveRatioBips == 10000); } }
